title: Migrate all legacy Python integration tests to Go native testing framework
context: Decommissioning the Python CLI entrypoints broke legacy Python integration tests due to missing module imports.
content: Instead of adapting the legacy Python test suite to call the compiled Go binary as a subprocess, completely delete the obsolete Python test files and implement their test cases directly inside Go's native testing library (`go test`). This removes Python execution dependencies, increases test performance, and keeps the test suite unified within the target language.
anti_patterns: Maintaining a hybrid Python/Go test runner environment which drags along Python interpreter and virtualenv overhead.
